title: camissonia brevipes subsp. pallidula
intro: Camissonia brevipes subsp. pallidula, also known as the Desert Gold or Pale Gold Poppy, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant's medicinal properties lie in its ability to produce a number of alkaloids, including berberine, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Camissonia brevipes subsp. pallidula include its potential to aid in the treatment of various infections, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying and processing the flowers, which can then be used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
